title: Naturally Ventilated (NV) Polyhouse

technical_specs.structure: GI tubular frame with UV polyfilm
technical_specs.suitable_climate: Mild to moderate — not for extreme heat zones

costs.note: Costs as per NHB Cost Norms. Actual may vary by state.

procedure: Apply online at nhb.gov.in for IPA (In Principle Approval)
Get IPA letter before starting any construction
Tie up with bank for project loan
Complete construction with bills and photographs
Bank releases subsidy as back-ended payment
warnings: Construction started BEFORE IPA will be rejected
Subsidy is released AFTER project completion, not upfront
Keep all bills and contractor receipts for verification
source: NHB Cost Norms PDF, NHB Scheme 1 Guidelines
verify_at: nhb.gov.in or nearest NHB regional office

title: Fan & Pad (Evaporative Cooling) Polyhouse

technical_specs.structure: GI tubular with cooling pads + exhaust fans
technical_specs.ventilation: Forced — fan pulls air through wet pads
technical_specs.temperature_control: Can reduce temp by 10–15°C vs outside
technical_specs.suitable_climate: Hot and semi-arid regions — Gujarat, Rajasthan, AP

costs.note: Higher cost due to electrical equipment. Power supply essential.

procedure: Confirm reliable power supply before applying
Submit bills and geo-tagged photos to NHB
warnings: Power cuts will affect crop quality — consider backup generator
Higher operating cost than NV polyhouse
Only viable if market price justifies the extra ₹22L/acre investment

income.note: Price varies significantly by variety and market. Red-fleshed commands premium.

subsidy.scheme: NHM (National Horticulture Mission) via state govt
subsidy.note: Check with state horticulture department — varies by state

suitability.ideal_for: Farmers with low water availability seeking long-term income

title: Capsicum (Coloured/Hybrid) in Polyhouse

technical_specs.support_system: V-shape or single stem training on wire
technical_specs.irrigation: Drip only — flood irrigation will cause disease

costs.note: Seed cost only. Add polyhouse cost separately.

income.note: Coloured capsicum (red/yellow) commands 3x price of green. Always grow coloured varieties in polyhouse.

critical_tips: Never grow in open field — quality drops, price collapses
Coloured varieties ONLY in polyhouse — green capsicum not viable
Maintain EC strictly — excess fertilizer burns roots
Harvest at colour-break stage for best shelf life

title: NHB Scheme 1 — Commercial Horticulture Development
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
critical_rules: IPA (In Principle Approval) MUST be obtained BEFORE starting construction
Project MUST be linked to a scheduled bank
Subsidy is back-ended — paid AFTER project completion
Land must be farmer's own OR 10-year registered lease
application_process: Step 2: Apply online at nhb.gov.in for IPA
Step 3: NHB reviews and issues IPA letter (takes 30–60 days)
Step 4: Approach bank with IPA letter for project loan
Step 5: Bank sanctions loan and sends letter to NHB
Step 6: Begin construction ONLY after IPA + bank sanction
Step 7: Complete project and submit geo-tagged photos + bills
Step 9: Subsidy released directly to bank loan account
common_mistakes: Starting construction before IPA — AUTOMATIC DISQUALIFICATION
Not maintaining bills — leads to rejection at verification
Using uncertified vendors — structure must meet NHB specs
Applying without bank linkage — scheme is credit-linked
verify_at: nhb.gov.in — scheme details may be updated annually
